mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during economic expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 5-day forward returns in > 55% of expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above the threshold consistently precede positive 20-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, supporting corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS increase and UNRATE decrease signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue expansion, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarterly revisions show zero or negative correlation with subsequent 20-day SPX direction over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising consumer prices indicates stagflationary pressures that compress profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM change < 0.1% and CPIAUCSL MoM change > 0.3%
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling INDPRO and rising CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered cases.
